title: Past simple: regular -ed
question: I ___ my room yesterday.
explanation: Yesterday = past. Add -ed to clean → cleaned.

title: Past simple: regular -ed
question: She ___ to the park last Sunday.
explanation: Last Sunday = past. Walk → walked.

title: Past simple: regular -ed
question: They ___ the movie last night.
explanation: Last night = past. Watch → watched.

title: Past simple: regular -ed
question: We ___ dinner at home yesterday.
explanation: Yesterday = past. Cook → cooked.

title: Past simple: regular -ed
question: He ___ his homework after school.
explanation: After school (past event). Finish → finished.

title: Past simple: spelling
question: She ___ the bus this morning.
explanation: Stop ends in consonant-vowel-consonant. Double the p → stopped.

title: Past simple: spelling
question: I ___ my friend to the party.
explanation: Invite ends in -e. Just add -d → invited.

title: Past simple: irregular
question: I ___ breakfast at 7 a.m.
explanation: Have is irregular. The past simple is had.

title: Past simple: irregular
question: She ___ to school by bus.
explanation: Go is irregular. The past simple is went.

title: Past simple: irregular
question: We ___ a great film on TV.
explanation: See is irregular. The past simple is saw.

title: Past simple: irregular
question: He ___ me a nice present.
explanation: Give is irregular. The past simple is gave.

title: Past simple: irregular
question: They ___ home at ten o'clock.
explanation: Come is irregular. The past simple is came.

title: Past simple: negative
question: She ___ the answer yesterday.
explanation: Past negative = didn't + base form. Didn't know.

title: Past simple: negative
question: I ___ to work yesterday.
explanation: Past negative = didn't + base form. Didn't go.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: The meeting is ___ Monday.
explanation: Days of the week use on. On Monday.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: I wake up ___ 7 o'clock.
explanation: Clock times use at. At 7 o'clock.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: My birthday is ___ March.
explanation: Months use in. In March.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: We have a party ___ Saturday evening.
explanation: Day + time of day uses on. On Saturday evening.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: He was born ___ 1995.
explanation: Years use in. In 1995.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: The shop closes ___ night.
explanation: We say at night (fixed expression).

title: Prepositions: at/in/on place
question: She is ___ home.
explanation: We say at home (fixed expression).

title: Prepositions: at/in/on place
question: The book is ___ the table.
explanation: On = touching a surface. The book is on the table.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on place
question: He lives ___ London.
explanation: Cities use in. He lives in London.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on place
question: I'll meet you ___ the bus stop.
explanation: A specific point/location uses at. At the bus stop.

title: Prepositions: at/in/on place
question: The keys are ___ my bag.
explanation: Inside something = in. In my bag.

title: Possessives: 's
question: This is ___ car.
explanation: Add 's to a name to show possession. Maria's car.

title: Possessives: 's
question: My ___ house is very big.
explanation: One brother who owns the house → brother's (singular possessive).

title: Possessives: 's
question: The ___ toys are on the floor.
explanation: Children is already plural. Add 's → children's.

title: Possessives: of
question: The name ___ is very long.
explanation: For things (not people), we often use of. The name of the street.

title: Possessives: 's vs of
question: What is your ___?
explanation: For people, use 's. Your sister's name.

title: Object pronouns
question: Can you help ___?
explanation: After a verb, use the object pronoun. Help me (not I).

title: Object pronouns
question: I saw ___ at the shop.
explanation: After a verb, use the object pronoun. I saw her.

title: Object pronouns
question: Please give ___ the book.
explanation: After a verb, use the object pronoun. Give him.

title: Object pronouns
question: She called ___ yesterday.
explanation: After a verb, use the object pronoun. She called us.

title: Object pronouns
question: I don't know ___.
explanation: After a verb, use the object pronoun. I don't know them.

title: Object pronouns
question: This present is for ___.
explanation: After a preposition, use the object pronoun. For you.

title: Object pronouns
question: Can you sit next to ___?
explanation: After a preposition (next to), use the object pronoun. Next to me.

title: Countable/uncountable: some/any
question: There aren't ___ eggs in the box.
explanation: In negative sentences, use any with countable nouns. There aren't any eggs.

title: Countable/uncountable: some/any
question: I have ___ apples.
explanation: In positive sentences, use some. Some apples.

title: Countable/uncountable: some/any
question: There isn't ___ bread left.
explanation: In negative sentences, use any. There isn't any bread.

title: Countable/uncountable: some/any
question: I don't want ___ sugar in my coffee.
explanation: In negative sentences, use any with uncountable nouns. Sugar is uncountable, so a/an/many are impossible.

title: Countable/uncountable: some/any
question: I don't have ___ money.
explanation: In negative sentences, use any. I don't have any money.

title: Countable/uncountable: much/many
question: How ___ books do you have?
explanation: Books are countable. Use many with countable nouns.

title: Countable/uncountable: much/many
question: There isn't ___ water in the glass.
explanation: Water is uncountable. Use much with uncountable nouns.

title: Past simple: question
question: ___ you see the match yesterday?
explanation: Past simple questions use Did + base form. Did you see?

title: Past simple: question
question: What time ___ she arrive yesterday?
explanation: Past simple questions use did + base form. What time did she arrive?

title: Prepositions: at/in/on time
question: I always read ___ the morning.
explanation: Parts of the day use in. In the morning.

title: Possessives: 's
question: Where are the ___?
explanation: One dog owns the toys. Dog's toys (singular possessive).

title: Object pronouns
question: Tell ___ the news, please.
explanation: After a verb, use the object pronoun. Tell her.

title: Countable/uncountable: a/some
question: I need ___ information.
explanation: Information is uncountable. You cannot say a information. Use some.
